question.text: Regarding net worth requirements for the derivatives segment, which statement is CORRECT?

answer.text: SEBI prescribes Rs. 3 crore net worth for clearing members and Rs. 1 crore for self-clearing members; no SEBI-prescribed net worth for trading members
Rs. 3 crore for trading members and Rs. 5 crore for clearing members
Rs. 50 lakhs for all member categories uniformly
No net worth requirement exists for any category of members

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Per the L.C. Gupta Committee and SEBI regulations, the minimum net worth for clearing members of the derivatives clearing corporation is Rs. 3 crore (Rs. 300 lakhs). For self-clearing members, it is Rs. 1 crore (Rs. 100 lakhs). SEBI has not specified any net worth requirement for trading members — their net worth criteria are decided by the exchanges.
**The Trap:** Rs. 3 crore is for CLEARING members, not trading members. This is a common exam trap. Also, Rs. 50 lakhs is the liquid net worth requirement (deposit with exchange), not the balance sheet net worth.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Net worth requirements for different member categories are frequently tested in Series VIII.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CM = Rs. 3 Cr, SCM = Rs. 1 Cr, TM = exchange-specified. Liquid net worth for all CMs = Rs. 50 lakhs minimum.
